title: Habeas adapter
description: Declarative definition of how to extract a user's own data from one service, in the user's live session. DATA, not code — no functions, no eval, no remote JS. This schema mirrors the runtime shape consumed by extension/src/runtime/inventory.js and enforced by extension/src/adapters/validate.js.

service.description: Short service slug used for sink folder/service naming.

domain.description: Registrable domain (eTLD+1) all hosts share, e.g. carrefour.es. If omitted it is derived from api.host.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the service (e.g. ES, FR, US) or 'global'. Used to filter the catalog.

crossDomainHosts.description: Explicit allowlist of EXTRA hosts/domains outside `domain` that this source reads from or replays the session to (e.g. a bank whose API is on another domain). Presence requires an off-site consent screen. No wildcards.

currency.description: ISO currency for normalized records; defaults to EUR.

categories.description: The categories this source can emit (used for sink compatibility). Must be from the canonical catalog — see docs/categories.md.

field.description: Source field (dotted path allowed) whose value selects a category.

match.description: URL match patterns for the login site where the session is captured.

mode.description: bearer = replay the captured JWT; cookie = rely on the browser's cookies (credentials:include). Endpoints may mix (e.g. cookie list + bearer PDF).

tokenMatch.description: Substring the captured Authorization must contain to be a real user token (e.g. eyJ).

replayHeaders.description: Captured request headers to replay when calling the API (may be empty for cookie auth).

captureHosts.description: Extra hosts (besides api.host and match) whose requests are observed to capture auth. All must resolve to `domain` or a crossDomainHosts entry.

from.description: 'html' → the list is server-rendered: fetch the page and parse items from embedded JSON (at itemsPath) or the largest HTML table (rows → objects keyed by column header, plus `href`).

paging.description: Pagination strategy. Defaults to offsets if offsetsPath present, else cursor if nextPath present, else none.

itemsPath.description: Dotted path to the array of items in the response (e.g. data.items). For from:html, the path into embedded page JSON (omit to parse an HTML table).

offsetsPath.description: [offsets] Dotted path to the offsets object merged into the next query.

nextPath.description: [cursor] Dotted path to the next cursor in the response.

cursorParam.description: [cursor] Query param name for the cursor (default: cursor).

pageParam.description: [page] Query param name for the page number (default: page).

offsetParam.description: [offset] Query param name for the item offset (default: offset).

offsetStep.description: [offset] Offset increment per page (the page size).

from.description: Query param name for the window start.

to.description: Query param name for the window end.

format.description: iso = full ISO timestamp (default), date = YYYY-MM-DD.

window.description: Lookback window for the range, e.g. 3y.

params.description: Static query params. `count` also drives the page-paging stop condition.

headers.description: Static headers the SPA sends for the list (e.g. dkt-ecom-origin).

referer.description: Referer to send per request (templated with {page}/{from}/{offset}) — for endpoints that only honour the offset when the Referer reflects the page. Set via declarativeNetRequest.

pdf.description: Per-document PDF. GET by default; some services generate it via POST with the invoice data as the body.

host.description: PDF host if different from api.host (still bound by the same-domain guard / crossDomainHosts).

referer.description: Referer header to send (templated with {internalId}) — for services that gate the PDF behind its detail page. Set via declarativeNetRequest.

detail.description: Per-document JSON detail (e.g. a full order). Saved as <id>.json. Preferred over a POST-generated PDF. Use `from:'list'` when the list endpoint already returns each item's full data (no extra request).

from.description: 'list' → the document IS the already-listed item's JSON (no path/request needed).

as.description: 'render' → open the path in a background tab, let the SPA render + fetch its data in the user's session, then capture the FINAL DOM self-contained (cross-browser; the faithful document for client-rendered pages). 'html' → fetch the path as a page and store it self-contained (server-rendered). 'invoice' → render a clean printable invoice from the detail JSON. All save a printable .html.

waitFor.description: [render] CSS selector to wait for before capturing (robust). If absent, waits waitMs.

waitMs.description: [render] Fixed wait in ms before capturing (default 3500) when waitFor is not set.

host.description: Detail host if different from api.host (same-domain guard applies).

headers.description: Static headers the SPA sends for this endpoint (e.g. dkt-ecom-origin).

referer.description: Referer header to send (templated with {internalId}) — for endpoints that validate it. Set via declarativeNetRequest.

document.description: The PRESENTABLE artifact (the archival/print document), separate from `detail` (structured data). A source may have BOTH; a sink chooses via accepts.artifacts. `as:render` (client-rendered page → captured), `as:html` (server-rendered page), `as:invoice` (generated from the detail JSON), or a plain PDF (path + optional method/body). Saved as <id>.html or <id>.pdf.

as.description: How to produce the document (see detail.as). Absent → a plain PDF at `path`.

waitFor.description: [render] CSS selector to wait for before capturing.

host.description: Host if different from api.host (same-domain guard applies).

fields.description: Map of normalized field name -> source field (dotted path). Recognized keys depend on `schema`: receipt (total, storeName, storeAddress, type, source), invoice (total, issuer, issuerAddress, number, type, source), transaction (amount, description, counterparty, direction, type, source), investment (instrument, isin, units, price, amount, operation, type).

schema.description: Target normalized schema: receipt@1 | invoice@1 | transaction@1 | investment@1.
